text:
Herein we present the synthesis of various heterocyclic compounds using 1-(2-hydroxyphenyl)-3-(4-methylphenyl)prop-2-en-1-one (chalcone 3) as parent molecule by applying pressurized microwave irradiation as green energy in the laboratory. Reactions of the parent chalcone 3 with ethyl cyanoacetate, acetylacetone and thioglycolic acid under pressurized microwave condition at 70 °C have given 2-hydroxyphenylcyanopyridone, 2-hydroxyphenyl acetylcyclohexanone and thieno[2,3-c]chromen-4-one respectively. Similar chromen-4-one derivative was obtained by oxidative cyclization of the parent chalcone 3 with hydrogen peroxide under stirring. The FT-IR, 1H NMR, 13C NMR and mass spectral data have been given to confirm the newly synthesized compounds in solid state as well as in solution.

Compounds derived from oxidative cycloaddition of activated azomethine ylides were found to exhibit enhanced free radical scavenging inhibition by means of in vitro biological experiments. This was validated by demonstrating that substitution by hydroxyl moiety could elevate their antioxidant properties than that of Vitamin C. Further studies that utilize the docking of compound 12 into the binding sites of protein structures of PDB ID: 1DH2 and 3RP8 have showed better binding energy values and lesser bond length than ascorbic acid. Additional calculations performed using the DFT/B3LYP/6-31G(d,p) method has enabled us to understand the optimized geometric structures of the heterocycles as well as predicted some physical descriptors of interest. Single-crystal X-ray diffraction analysis on compound 12 affirmed its proposed structure while giving comprehensive information about the hydrogen bond interactions and electrostatic features as described via Hirshfeld surface analysis. Notably, a good accordance between experimental and simulated data, namely the structural parameters as well as FT-IR and 1 H NMR spectra of the crystals was established, attesting to the reliability of the computed structures.
headline: Fully Human Written
prediction: We believe that this document is fully human-written
